stationFilter: agents/prime + agents/tempo identity bundles inside wrkstrm collective

findings.message: Prime's identity.json now binds core-entity-set-v1.0.0 via schemaSetRefs; identity-schemas v0.4.0; persona.ikigai.sourcePath points at prime.イキガイ.md.
Prime's organism.json now binds core-entity-set-v1.0.0 at the top level; organism-schemas v0.5.0; kind: agent; embodiment: digital; intelligenceSubstrates: [silicon].
Tempo's identity.json now binds core-entity-set-v1.0.0 via schemaSetRefs; persona.ikigai.sourcePath points at tempo.イキガイ.md; previously had only identity.json + organism.json + 2 breadcrumb stubs.
Tempo's organism.json now binds core-entity-set-v1.0.0; agenda.json + chronicle.json authored from scratch (didn't exist before).
All 4 breadcrumb stubs (created 2026-04-05 to flag .clia/agents/clia/ legacy paths) trashed since the cutover is now resolved in the committed identity files.

extensions.note: PatrolReceipt shape is opinionated about koma patrols (factory mint + conveyor load); this receipt stretches the semantics by recording a manual claude-authored commissioning as if it were a patrol. digikomaSlug: claude + digikomaVersion: 0 mark this as a non-koma source. Future doctrine question: do manual audits warrant a separate receipt shape, or is the slight semantic stretch acceptable? Tracked in the evidenced-bead-closures-doctrine bead.
